zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de la Pole於1302年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親Sir William de la Pole33歲，母親Elena31歲。他於大約1325年與Katherine Norwich在英國越可爾赫爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1339年被列為baron of the exchequer。他去世於1366年6月21日在英國越可爾赫爾享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de la Pole于1302年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲Sir William de la Pole33岁，母亲Elena31岁。他于大约1325年与Katherine Norwich在英国越可爾赫爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1339年被列为baron of the exchequer。他去世于1366年6月21日在英国越可爾赫爾享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de la Pole was born in 1302, in Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir William de la Pole, was 33 and his mother, Elena, was 31. He married Katherine Norwich about 1325, in Hull, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. In 1339, at the age of 37, his occupation is listed as baron of the exchequer. He died on 21 June 1366, in Hull, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de la Pole est né en 1302, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de la Pole, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Elena, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Norwich environ 1325, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. En 1339, à l’âge de 37 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est baron of the exchequer. Il est décédé le 21 juin 1366, à Hull, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de la Pole 1302 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de la Pole 33 und seine Mutter Elena 31. Er hat um 1325 in Hull, Yorkshire, England Katherine Norwich geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1339, als er 37 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als baron of the exchequer aufgeführt. Er starb am 21. Juni 1366 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Hull, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Pole è nato il nel 1302 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir William de la Pole, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Elena, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Katherine Norwich circa 1325, nel Hull, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Nel 1339, all'età di 37 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come baron of the exchequer. Morì il 21 giugno 1366, nel Hull, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Poleが1302年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William de la Poleは33歳，母Elenaは31歳であった。約1325年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハルでKatherine Norwichと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1339年，37歳の時，職業はbaron of the exchequerと記録されている。彼は1366年6月21日にイギリスヨークシュアイアハルで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Pole(이)가 1302년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de la Pole(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Elena(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1325년 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 Katherine Norwich(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1339년, 37세의 나이로 그의 직업은 baron of the exchequer이었다. 그는 1366년 6월 21일 영국 요르크실르 훌에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Pole nasceu em 1302, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de la Pole, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Elena, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Norwich aproximadamente 1325, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Em 1339, com 37 anos, sua profissão aparece como baron of the exchequer. Ele faleceu em 21 de junho de 1366, em Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de la Pole родился в 1302 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William de la Pole было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elena, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Norwich около 1325 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. В 1339 году, в возрасте 37 лет,  он работал по профессии baron of the exchequer. Он умер 21 июня 1366 г. в местности хулл, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de la Pole nació en 1302, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de la Pole, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Elena, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Katherine Norwich aproximadamente 1325, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. En 1339, a la edad de 37 años, su ocupación registrada era baron of the exchequer. Murió el 21 de junio de 1366, en Hull, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años.
